changes.title: Standups with < or > characters post correctly
items: Standup text containing characters like < or > (for example a pull request title) no longer breaks the posted standup summary when your standup is submitted through a connected AI agent.

changes.title: See who's done their standup at a glance
items: /dd-team-members now shows each member's status — submitted, not yet, on leave, notifications, and role.
/dd-team-list lists members under each team with the same at-a-glance status.

changes.title: A shared daily-dose-bot channel for your workspace
items: Your workspace now gets a dedicated daily-dose-bot channel, and members are automatically added when they join a team.

changes.title: Release announcements in your workspace
items: New features and improvements are now announced right in your daily-dose-bot channel, so your team always knows what's new.

changes.title: Standup updates are now labeled correctly in the channel
items: When you update your standup after the team summary has posted, the thread reply now shows "🔄 Standup Updated" instead of being mislabeled "🕐 Late Submission"

changes.title: Let your AI agent draft and preview your standup
items: Your connected AI agent can now pull together what you worked on from your own tools (git, Jira, ClickUp, Trello), draft your standup, and show you a preview before anything is submitted
Review the draft — including whether it would overwrite an existing entry or count as late — and your agent only submits after you confirm

changes.title: Submit standups from any AI agent
items: Connect Daily Dose to Claude Desktop, Cursor, or any MCP-compatible AI agent — generate a personal token at /mcp-tokens, paste it into your AI tool, and submit or update your standup without leaving your workflow
Tokens are tied to your Slack identity, revocable at any time, and expire after 90 days

changes.title: Team leads can review standups from their AI agent
items: Team admins and owners can now pull a team's standup summary — who responded, who hasn't, and who's on leave — directly from any connected AI agent
Look up a single teammate's update for any date without opening Slack

changes.title: Run standups from your AI agent
items: Team admins and owners can now post a team's standup summary or a single member's update, and send standup or follow-up reminders — all from any connected AI agent
Every action re-checks your team permissions, so a token only ever does what you're already allowed to do

changes.title: Connect your AI agent with one Slack click
items: Supported AI clients (Claude Desktop, Cursor, VS Code, and more) can now connect to Daily Dose just by adding the server — sign in with Slack in your browser and you're done, with no token to copy
See and disconnect your connected AI clients anytime from the token page

changes.title: Anyone can create a team — with admin approval
items: You no longer need to be added to the organization first: just run /dd-team-create and you'll be onboarded automatically
If you're a regular member, your new team is sent to an organization admin for a quick Approve or Reject — you'll be notified the moment they decide, and standups start as soon as it's approved
Organization owners and admins still get instant team creation, no approval needed

changes.title: A brand-new website landing page
items: The Daily Dose homepage has been completely redesigned with a refined dark look — see what a day with Daily Dose feels like, browse features, stats, and FAQs at a glance
Animations are smooth and scroll-triggered, and automatically tone down if your device prefers reduced motion
Prefer the old homepage? It's still available at /v1

changes.title: Contact form now sends your message
items: Submitting the contact form on the website now delivers your message to the team instead of silently doing nothing

changes.title: Team timezones are validated when you set them
items: Setting or updating a team's timezone now catches typos (like America/NewYork) right away, instead of silently breaking the team's standup schedule

changes.title: Reminder opt-out is respected
items: Turning off notifications with /dd-standup-reminder notify=off now stops standup reminder and follow-up DMs, instead of still messaging you every day

changes.title: Standups with special characters and long content post reliably
items: Updates containing characters like &, <, or > no longer break the posted summary's formatting
Very long blockers no longer prevent a standup from posting

changes.title: Posting a member's standup by @mention now works
items: Mentioning a member in /dd-standup-post or /dd-standup-preview now correctly targets that person instead of the whole team

changes.title: Preview and post a single member's standup
items: Admins can preview or post one team member's standup by mentioning them, e.g. /dd-standup-preview @alice or /dd-standup-post @alice
Posting a member adds their update as a reply under the day's team standup, and posts the team summary first if it isn't up yet

changes.title: Organization admins get admin access
items: Organization admins can now run admin standup commands, alongside owners and team admins

changes.title: Late standups post reliably
items: Fixed late standup submissions failing to post when an update contained long content such as pasted links

changes.title: Standup form opens reliably
items: Fixed the standup form failing to open when your previous update included indented or nested bullet/numbered lists

changes.title: More reliable standups
items: Fixed errors that could stop standup posting, reminders, and previews from working
Long updates with lots of links no longer fail to post

changes.title: Slack App Home Tab
items: The Daily Dose bot now has a Home tab in Slack with a quick welcome and one-click buttons to the intro video, website, docs, and changelog
Includes a get-started checklist and a handy commands cheat sheet so new teammates can ramp up without leaving Slack

changes.title: Get-Started Video on Homepage
items: The homepage now features an embedded demo video showing Daily Dose in action — reminders, standup submission, slash commands, and team summaries
Click the fullscreen button on the video to watch with audio

changes.title: Stricter Time Validation
items: `/dd-team-create` and `/dd-team-update` now reject malformed times like `99:99` right away with a clear message, instead of failing silently later
Times are normalized on save — entering `9:5` is accepted and stored as `09:05`

changes.title: Friendlier Error Messages
items: Unexpected errors now show a short message with a reference code instead of raw internal details
Clear, expected messages (like "You cannot promote yourself") are still shown in full

changes.title: No More Duplicate Standup Posts
items: When the posting job ran twice for the same team and day, the bot used to post the standup summary again and lose the thread for late replies — it now detects the existing post and skips

changes.title: Promote Members to Admin
items: Organization owners and admins can now promote a member to admin with `/dd-org-promote @user`
Add a team name to scope the promotion to a single team — e.g. `/dd-org-promote @john Engineering` makes them a team admin in Engineering
Accepts the same target formats as the suspend commands: `@mention`, raw Slack user ID, or bare username
Safety guards prevent self-promotion, promoting an existing admin, or trying to promote the organization owner

changes.title: `/dd-team-list` Now Respects Your Role
items: Members and team admins now see only the teams they belong to when running `/dd-team-list`
Organization owners, organization admins, and super admins continue to see every team in the organization
The response heading changes accordingly — "Your teams:" for members vs "Teams in <Organization>:" for admins

changes.title: Suspend Already-Deactivated Members
items: Suspend commands now accept a Slack username or user ID, so admins can clean up members who were already deactivated in Slack and can no longer be @-mentioned

changes.title: Suspend Inactive Members
items: Team admins can now suspend a member from a single team with `/dd-team-suspend @user [team-name]` (and reactivate with `/dd-team-unsuspend`)
Organization owners and admins can suspend a member across the entire organization with `/dd-org-suspend @user` (reactivate with `/dd-org-unsuspend`)
Suspended members cannot submit standups, stop receiving reminders, and are excluded from posted summaries
Daily Dose now automatically suspends members when they're deactivated in Slack — no manual cleanup needed
Safety guards prevent suspending the only active admin of a team or accidentally changing an organization owner's status

changes.title: `/dd-standup` No Longer Errors in DMs
items: Running `/dd-standup` without a team name in a DM with the bot (or in a non-team channel) used to return a 500 error if you were on more than five teams
Now it shows a clean usage hint listing your available teams

changes.title: Cleaner Error Messages Across All Commands
items: All slash-command error responses now share a consistent format: a primary message plus bulleted suggestions
Affects usage hints, validation errors, and 'team not found' messages across team, leave, holiday, standup, and preferences commands

changes.title: View Your Standup History
items: New `/dd-standup-history` command lets you privately review your own standup submissions across all your teams
Run with no arguments to see your most recent submitted day
Pass a single date (`/dd-standup-history 2024-12-20`) to see that day, or two dates for a range
Date order doesn't matter — the bot figures it out

changes.title: GitHub Repository Link
items: The 'Add to Slack' button now links to the GitHub repository
Easily access the source code directly from the homepage

changes.title: Slash Commands Now Work Reliably
items: Fixed a critical issue where all /dd-* slash commands failed with 'app did not respond'
Commands now respond immediately and consistently in all environments

changes.title: Web Application Enhancements
items: Added favicon and app icons for better branding across all devices
Progressive Web App (PWA) support with manifest file
iOS home screen icon support for a native app-like experience
Multiple icon sizes for optimal display on any device

changes.title: Development Tools
items: Docker Compose configuration for easier local development setup
Git Town configuration for streamlined branch management
Environment setup examples for admin configuration

changes.title: Bulk Standup Management
items: Send standup reminders to all teams at once with a single command
Post standup summaries for all teams simultaneously
Safety confirmation prompt before bulk operations
Preview which teams will be affected before proceeding
Detailed success and failure reports for each team

changes.title: Improved Administrative Tools
items: Administrators can now manage standups across all teams more efficiently
Better error handling ensures operations continue even if one team fails
Preview mode allows testing before sending actual messages

changes.title: Holiday System Improvements
items: Fixed an issue preventing holiday schedules from working correctly
Resolved errors that were blocking standup automation during holidays

changes.title: Organization-Specific Holidays
items: Each organization can now manage its own holiday schedule independently
Holidays are no longer shared across different workspaces
Better support for teams in different countries and regions

changes.title: Better Notification Controls
items: Team admins can now turn off all standup notifications if they prefer
Clearer documentation on how notification settings work
Better control over when you receive reminders and alerts

changes.title: Bug Fixes
items: Fixed preferences command to work as documented
Improved command documentation accuracy

changes.title: React Web Application
items: Home page with hero section, features, and how it works
Comprehensive documentation with search functionality
Changelog page with version history
Contact form with functional form submission
Theme toggle for dark/light mode support
Lazy loading for pages with loading fallbacks
Global page transitions and scroll-to-top functionality

changes.title: Developer Tools & Infrastructure
items: GitHub issue and pull request templates
Organization administrator management script (addOrgAdmin.js)
Documentation search functionality
Lucide icons integration for UI components
Enhanced responsiveness with sticky sidebar

changes.title: Architecture & Infrastructure
items: Removed legacy static HTML routes and basicAuth middleware
Replaced multiple static routes with single-page application (SPA)
Refactored documentation sidebar navigation to use external docs.json
Updated Node.js version requirement to 20.19+ or 22.12+
Improved Slack manifest update script with better error handling
Enhanced Tailwind CSS configuration and styling
Restructured UI components for theme-aware styling

changes.title: Deprecated
items: Web frontend application static files
Legacy HTML routes and basic auth middleware
Unnecessary console log statements

changes.title: Manual Standup Controls
items: Send standup reminders manually anytime, not just at scheduled times
Post standup summaries for any past date
Preview standup summaries before posting them to the channel
Send follow-up reminders to team members who haven't responded yet

changes.title: Smarter Permissions
items: Team admins can now manage their teams more easily
Commands are context-aware - no need to specify team name when you're already in the team channel
Better error messages when you don't have permission to perform an action

changes.title: Better Late Submission Handling
items: Late standup submissions now appear in the channel even if no summary was posted yet
Your late submission won't be lost - it creates a new post if needed
Multiple late submissions are neatly organized in a thread

changes.title: Admin Leave Management
items: Team admins can now set leave for team members
Manage leaves for your entire team from Slack
Use @mentions to quickly set someone's leave status
View all upcoming leaves for your team members

changes.title: Smarter Standup Posting
items: Bot now skips posting if no one on the team is available
Team members who opt out of mentions won't be shown in 'not responded' lists
Cleaner standup summaries with better formatting

changes.title: Initial Release
items: Automated daily standup reminders
Team management commands
Leave tracking system
Timezone-aware scheduling
Work day configuration
Standup response collection via modals
Team standup summaries
Late submission threading
User preference management
Admin role management
Multi-tenant organization support
PostgreSQL database with Prisma ORM
PM2 process management
Health check endpoint
Comprehensive documentation
